name: Design by Contract
def: Specifying preconditions, postconditions and invariants as enforceable obligations between caller and callee.
problem: Pin responsibility for correctness precisely and detect violations close to their cause.
mechanism: Each routine states what callers must guarantee (preconditions), what it then guarantees (postconditions), and invariants the type always upholds; violations fail fast.
benefits: Bugs surface at the violation point
limits: Few mainstream languages enforce it natively
Runtime checks cost (often disabled in release)
failures: Contracts compiled out, masking real violations
Treating defensive checks as a substitute for design
myths: "Same as defensive programming" — DbC assigns responsibility; it doesn’t check redundantly everywhere
